message:
test(perf): add PI_PERF_STRICT mode for binary size release artifact gate

Add a strict mode (PI_PERF_STRICT=1) to binary_size_check() that fails
closed when the release binary is missing, rather than silently skipping.
This enables CI pipelines to enforce that performance regression tests
actually measure a release artifact instead of quietly passing without
one.

Changes:
- Add PI_PERF_STRICT_ENV constant and perf_strict_mode() /
  perf_strict_mode_from() helpers for parsing the env var
- Add binary_size_missing_release_outcome() that returns Ok(()) in
  non-strict mode (skip) or Err with diagnostic message in strict mode
  (panic with candidate paths and env var name for remediation)
- binary_size_check() now checks strict mode when release binary is
  missing and either skips or panics accordingly
- Structured logging for the strict-mode failure path includes
  strict_mode, env var name, and checked candidate paths

Tests:
- binary_size_missing_release_outcome_skips_when_not_strict
- binary_size_missing_release_outcome_fails_closed_when_strict
- perf_strict_mode_from_accepts_truthy_tokens (1, true, " True ")
- perf_strict_mode_from_rejects_non_truthy_tokens (None, "", 0, false, yes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
